title: Delicate Spring Canadian Potatoes Gratin
description: A paleo-friendly, wine-infused Canadian dish featuring Jersey Royal potatoes, perfect for a date night. This recipe combines the freshness of spring with the richness of a gratin.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, combine the sliced potatoes and enough cold water to cover them, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the potatoes are slightly tender.
Drain the potatoes and set them aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, for 4 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the dry white wine and chicken broth to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced by half.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of potatoes.
Pour some of the wine sauce over the potatoes, then sprinkle with some of the chopped parsley and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Repeat the layering process until all the ingredients have been used, finishing with a layer of potatoes on top.
Dot the top of the potatoes with the unsalted butter.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
